reviewDescription: Cross-cutting error handling review across entire codebase

findings.description: Hardcoded hotkey parse with .unwrap() - platform fallback hotkey 'Alt+F9' uses unwrap on parse_hotkey result. If the hardcoded string is invalid, this would panic the platform thread.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider using expect with a clear message explaining why this hardcoded value should always succeed, or validate at compile time.

findings.description: Hardcoded hotkey parse with .unwrap() - platform fallback hotkey 'Ctrl+Shift+S' uses unwrap on parse_hotkey result. Similar risk as Alt+F9 case.
findings.suggestedFix: Use expect with descriptive message or compile-time validation.

findings.description: Hotkey::from_str with .unwrap() - fallback hotkey registration uses unwrap. If the string format is invalid, registration thread would panic.
findings.suggestedFix: Use expect with message explaining fallback hotkey should always parse correctly.

findings.description: Chained .unwrap() calls on scaler.as_ref().unwrap() - three consecutive unwrap calls checking scaler properties. If scaler is None, this would panic during output processing.
findings.suggestedFix: Use pattern matching or if-let to safely handle scaler presence, with proper error propagation via bail! or return Err.

findings.description: .unwrap() on stream lookup - video stream lookup by index uses unwrap. If stream index is invalid (corrupted file), this would panic.
findings.suggestedFix: Use ok_or_else to convert None to error with context about the stream index and file.

findings.description: .unwrap() on audio stream lookup - similar to video stream case, audio stream by index uses unwrap.
findings.suggestedFix: Use ok_or_else with descriptive error message about the missing audio stream.

findings.description: .expect('scaler') - scaler creation uses expect but message is minimal. Better than unwrap but could be more descriptive.
findings.suggestedFix: Expand expect message to explain context: 'scaler should exist after successful init with format conversion'

findings.description: .expect('QueryPerformanceCounter should never fail') - QPC call uses expect. This is appropriate since QPC is documented to never fail on supported Windows.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - this is a correct use of expect for a documented invariant.

findings.description: .expect('PooledRgbaImage already consumed') - pool item consumption uses expect. This is an internal invariant violation detection.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider returning Result instead if this can occur from external misuse, but current design is acceptable for internal API contract.

findings.description: let _ = pattern on mutex lock failures - three consecutive 'let _ = self.shared.*.lock().map(...)' patterns. Lock poisoning is silently ignored, which could mask thread panic.
findings.suggestedFix: Handle lock poisoning explicitly - either log the error, or if lock poisoning indicates panic, propagate appropriately.

findings.description: let _ = handle.join() - thread join results are silently discarded. Thread panics during decode pipeline cleanup are not surfaced.
findings.suggestedFix: Log join errors or consider surfacing thread panic to user via error channel.

findings.description: let _ = platform_handle.update_recording_state(...) - multiple locations where tray icon update errors are silently ignored. Tray icon update failures are not surfaced.
findings.suggestedFix: At minimum log these failures for debugging; tray icon state mismatches could confuse users.

findings.description: let _ = self.quit() and let _ = handle.join() - platform thread cleanup errors silently ignored. Quit and thread join failures during shutdown are not surfaced.
findings.suggestedFix: Log cleanup errors during shutdown; panics in platform thread should be surfaced.

findings.description: let _ = health_tx.try_send(...) - encoder health event send failures silently ignored. If health channel is full/disconnected, fatal error notification is lost.
findings.suggestedFix: Health channel failures indicate serious state corruption; log or panic when critical health events cannot be sent.

findings.description: let _ = fatal_tx.try_send(...) - capture fatal error notifications use try_send with result discarded. If channel is full, fatal error is lost.
findings.suggestedFix: Fatal errors should always be communicated; consider blocking send or panic if channel cannot accept fatal message.

findings.description: let _ = progress_tx.send(...) - export progress notifications silently ignored. Progress channel failures during export are not surfaced.
findings.suggestedFix: Log progress send failures; user won't see progress but export continues, so this is acceptable but could be improved.

findings.description: let _ = progress_tx.send(ClipExportUpdate::Progress...) - SDK export progress updates silently ignored.
findings.suggestedFix: Similar to video_file.rs - logging would help debugging but export continues successfully.

findings.description: let _ = std::fs::remove_file/remove_dir_all(...) - cleanup file removal errors silently ignored. This is intentional cleanup behavior; failures are expected to be common.
findings.suggestedFix: This is appropriate for cleanup operations where failure should not affect primary outcome.

findings.description: let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&thumb_path) - thumbnail cleanup silently ignored. Intentional cleanup behavior.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - cleanup failures are expected and should not affect user experience.

findings.description: let _ = ictx.seek(ts, ..) - seek result silently ignored during video export. Seek failures could cause incorrect frame extraction.
findings.suggestedFix: Check seek result; log warning if seek fails as this affects output quality.

findings.description: let _ = input_ctx.seek(ts, ..) - SDK export seek result silently ignored. Same concern as sdk_ffmpeg_output.rs.
findings.suggestedFix: Check seek result and handle errors appropriately.

findings.description: Menu .append() calls using .ok() - tray menu item additions silently ignore failures. Tray menu creation failures result in incomplete menu.
findings.suggestedFix: Log failed menu item additions; user will see incomplete menu but app continues.

findings.description: Windows registry API calls using .ok() - RegOpenKeyExW, RegSetValueExW, RegCloseKey errors silently converted to Option. Autostart registration failures are not surfaced to user.
findings.suggestedFix: Log registry operation failures; autostart failure is non-critical but user may want to know.

findings.description: Multiple D3D11 API calls using .ok() - texture creation, copy, and GPU operations silently ignore failures. GPU operation failures are logged via warn but not propagated.
findings.suggestedFix: For critical texture operations, propagate errors via Result; for optional operations, current pattern is acceptable.

findings.description: ReleaseFrame().ok() - DXGI frame release errors silently ignored. Frame release failure could accumulate resources.
findings.suggestedFix: Log ReleaseFrame failures for debugging; resource accumulation could affect performance.

findings.description: CoInitializeEx().ok() - COM initialization failure silently ignored. Audio capture thread may fail if COM is not initialized.
findings.suggestedFix: COM initialization failure should be propagated as audio capture cannot work without it.

findings.description: CoInitializeEx result handled via .ok()? - pattern returns Option. This is combined with ? operator, so failure propagates correctly.
findings.suggestedFix: Pattern is correct - failure returns None which propagates as error.

findings.description: WASAPI device property access via .ok()? - chained .ok()? pattern for device info retrieval. Combined with ? operator so errors propagate.
findings.suggestedFix: Pattern is correct for chained optional operations - error propagates via ?.

findings.description: encoder.send_eof().ok() - EOF send during flush silently ignored. Flush is cleanup operation; failure is logged elsewhere.
findings.suggestedFix: Acceptable for cleanup operation; flush continues with partial data.

findings.description: DXGI access lost handling - CORRECT IMPLEMENTATION. DXGI_ERROR_ACCESS_LOST triggers reinitialization with exponential backoff (100ms to 5000ms max). Error count tracking with max_errors=10 threshold. Proper handling of secure desktop/UAC scenarios.
findings.analysisNotes: This is a well-implemented recovery pattern. The code detects access lost, logs warning, attempts reinit with backoff, and tracks consecutive errors. After 10 consecutive failures, stops capture and reports fatal error.

findings.description: Pipeline health enforcement - CORRECT IMPLEMENTATION. enforce_health() polls encoder and capture for fatal errors. On fatal detection: logs error, transitions to Faulted state, calls stop() for clean shutdown, returns fatal reason to caller. Fail-closed pattern prevents silent failures.
findings.analysisNotes: Proper fail-closed behavior - pipeline is stopped on any component failure, ensuring no partial recording state persists.

findings.description: Config change rollback - CORRECT IMPLEMENTATION. apply_config() saves old config before restart, validates new config, attempts restart. On failure: rolls back to old config, recreates buffer, restarts with old settings, returns error. CRITICAL rollback failure is logged but not propagated as error.
findings.analysisNotes: Rollback logic is comprehensive but double-failure case (rollback also fails) is logged as error but not returned as Result. App may be in inconsistent state after rollback failure.

findings.description: Rollback failure handling incomplete - when both new config start and rollback start fail, only error is logged. AppState may be in broken state (no recording, stale buffer).
findings.suggestedFix: Return a special error type indicating 'rollback also failed' so caller can decide to show critical error or attempt full restart.

findings.description: Hardware encoder fallback - CORRECT IMPLEMENTATION. should_fallback_to_software_encoder() detects NVENC/AMF/QSV runtime failures and switches to libx265. Fallback triggers during calibration and export phases.
findings.analysisNotes: Comprehensive fallback detection covering driver errors, initialization failures, and runtime errors. Allows export to complete even if hardware encoder fails mid-process.

findings.description: Encoder error tolerance - consecutive encode errors tracked with MAX_CONSECUTIVE_ENCODE_ERRORS=8 threshold. Individual errors logged but don't stop encoder immediately. After threshold, fatal health event sent and encoder stops.
findings.analysisNotes: Good balance between resilience (tolerating transient errors) and safety (stopping after consistent failure pattern).

findings.description: Encoder initialization failure handling - init failure sends fatal health event and returns error. Good pattern for critical startup failures.
findings.suggestedFix: Pattern is correct - initialization failures are immediately fatal.

findings.description: Encoder lookup uses ok_or_else - correct pattern converting None to EncodeError. Good error propagation pattern for encoder availability.
findings.analysisNotes: Positive example - proper use of ok_or_else with descriptive error messages.

findings.description: CLI pipe encoder uses ok_or_else for codec lookup and state validation. Good pattern for required resource errors.
findings.analysisNotes: Positive example - proper error propagation for required resources.

findings.description: Audio stream lookup uses ok_or_else with descriptive error. Good pattern for stream access.
findings.analysisNotes: Positive example - proper use of ok_or_else for stream validation.

findings.description: Multiple .unwrap() and .expect() calls in test code - these are acceptable in test context where failures should be obvious and immediate.
findings.analysisNotes: Test code appropriately uses unwrap/expect for clarity. No production risk.

findings.description: .unwrap() in doc example code blocks - these are in 'no_run' or 'ignore' doc examples and don't affect production.
findings.analysisNotes: Doc examples appropriately use unwrap for clarity. No production risk.

findings.description: .expect() and .unwrap() in build.rs - build script uses expect/unwrap for icon generation and shader compilation. Build failures should stop compilation.
findings.analysisNotes: Build script appropriately uses expect for critical operations. Build failure is desired outcome if resources missing.

systemicPatterns.description: Critical health/fatal events sent via try_send with results discarded. If channel is full or disconnected, critical error notifications are lost. This pattern appears in capture (fatal_tx), encoder (health_tx), and export (progress_tx).
systemicPatterns.impact: High - lost notifications can mask critical failures

systemicPatterns.description: Mutex lock poisoning is silently ignored via let _ = lock.map(...). If a thread panics while holding the lock, the lock becomes poisoned and subsequent access returns Err. This is silently discarded.
systemicPatterns.impact: Medium - thread panics during lock holding are masked

systemicPatterns.description: Thread join results are silently discarded during cleanup. Panics in worker threads are not surfaced to caller or user.
systemicPatterns.impact: Medium - thread panics are silently swallowed during cleanup

systemicPatterns.description: Windows API calls frequently use .ok() to convert HRESULT errors to Option, which silently discards failures. Common in DXGI, WASAPI, and registry operations.
systemicPatterns.impact: Medium - Windows API failures are silently ignored, may cause partial functionality

systemicPatterns.description: Hardcoded hotkey strings use unwrap on parse, assuming hardcoded values are always valid. While likely true, adds risk if strings are modified incorrectly.
systemicPatterns.impact: Low - hardcoded values are trusted but could fail on modification

systemicPatterns.description: POSITIVE: DXGI access lost handling is well-implemented with exponential backoff, error counting, and proper reinit flow.
POSITIVE: Hardware encoder failures trigger automatic fallback to software encoding. should_fallback_to_software_encoder covers multiple failure modes.
POSITIVE: Pipeline uses fail-closed pattern - any component failure triggers clean shutdown and state transition to Faulted.
POSITIVE: Encoder and stream lookups use ok_or_else with descriptive errors. Good pattern for required resources.

keyStrengths: No panic! usage in production code
Well-implemented DXGI access lost recovery with backoff
Hardware encoder fallback covers multiple failure modes
Pipeline fail-closed pattern prevents silent failures
Encoder consecutive error tolerance before fatal shutdown
Config rollback on failed restart attempts
keyWeaknesses: Critical health/fatal events can be lost if channels full
Thread panics during cleanup are silently swallowed
Mutex lock poisoning is silently ignored
Windows API failures frequently ignored via .ok()
Hardcoded hotkey strings use risky unwrap
Rollback double-failure case not surfaced to caller
